title: Packing Code to DBMS Storage Format Mappings
breadcrumbs: Packing Code to DBMS Storage Format Mappings
text: Packing Code to DBMS Storage Format Mappings Uniface automatically maps model-level packing codes to connector-level packing codes, so the connector only needs to be aware of the connector-level packing codes. This mapping: Enables the connector to restrict the number of packing codes that it supports, without restricting the portability of Uniface applications. At the model-level, the application designer has a choice of several hundred combinations of Uniface data type and packing code for every field definition. Reduces the complexity of the connector by mapping model-level packing codes to preferred connector-level packing codes. The connector does not generally need to perform data conversions, since it only supports the packing codes that correspond directly to a DBMS storage format. Uniface handles the conversion for you. There is a distinction between original connector-level packing codes and redefined connector-level packing codes. The connector must specify a mapping table in uinfo->upfmt[] in the Info connector request. This table specifies the mapping from original connector- level packing codes to redefined connector-level packing codes. Every DBMS connector is responsible for mapping the redefined connector-level packing codes to storage formats recognized by the API of the DBMS. The following illustration shows the complete mapping process: For the application designer, the illustrated process is invisible. In the rest of the Uniface documentation, there is no distinction made between the application model-level packing codes and the connector-level packing codes. More Information For packing code mappings of the DBMS connectors Uniface provides, see the appropriate connector documentation in Database Connectors . For connector-level packing codes, see Connector-Level Packing Codes . For possible redefinitions of connector-level packing codes, see the uinfo->upfmt field in uinfo . Was this topic helpful?
